title: Northland in July
meta_desc: Discover Northland with kids in July. Enjoy mild winter weather, explore Cape Reinga, Ninety Mile Beach and ancient kauri forests with fewer crowds and lower prices, ideal for families with kids aged 4+
intro: Northland is a fantastic winter destination for families, with mild temperatures and plenty of sunshine. In July, you can enjoy the region's stunning natural beauty without the crowds, making it an ideal time to explore with kids. With a daily budget of $200-380 for a family of four, you can experience the best of Northland's subtropical north.

sections.heading: Weather and What's On
sections.body:
In July, Northland's winter weather is mild, with average highs of 15°C and lows of 6°C. It's a great time to enjoy outdoor activities like sandboarding on Ninety Mile Beach or exploring the ancient kauri forests. The school holidays are over, so you can expect fewer crowds and lower prices for accommodations and activities.

The Cape Reinga Lighthouse and Waitangi Treaty Grounds are open year-round, but be sure to check the Kauri Museum's winter hours before visiting. The Hokianga Harbour dunes are also a great spot to explore, with stunning views of the harbour and surrounding landscape.
list: Cape Reinga Lighthouse: open daily from 9am to 5pm, $12.50 per adult, $6.50 per child (5-15 years)
Kauri Museum: open daily from 9am to 5pm, $25 per adult, $10 per child (5-15 years)

sections.heading: Best of Northland in July
sections.body:
July is a great time to experience Northland's natural beauty without the crowds. Take a drive along Ninety Mile Beach, go sandboarding or simply enjoy the stunning views. The Cape Reinga walkway is a must-do, with a 30-minute return walk to the very tip of New Zealand.

The Kauri Museum is also a great place to visit, with a vast collection of kauri timber and artifacts. The Waitangi Treaty Grounds are a significant historical site, and a great place to learn about New Zealand's history and culture.
list: Cape Reinga walkway: free, 30-minute return walk
Ninety Mile Beach: free to walk and watch, 4WD only at most points

sections.heading: Crowd Levels and Booking Ahead
sections.body:
In July, Northland is relatively quiet, making it easy to get around and enjoy the region's attractions without the crowds. However, it's still a good idea to book ahead for popular activities and accommodations, especially during the weekends or school holidays.

Be sure to book your accommodations and activities in advance to avoid missing out, especially if you're planning to visit during the weekends or school holidays. Prices for accommodations and activities are generally lower in July, making it a great time to visit Northland on a budget.
list: Book accommodations and activities in advance, especially during weekends or school holidays
Check prices and availability for popular attractions like the Cape Reinga Lighthouse and Kauri Museum

sections.heading: Practical Tips for Families
sections.body: Northland is a great destination for families, with plenty of outdoor activities and attractions to enjoy. Here are some practical tips to keep in mind when visiting with kids:
list: Pack warm clothing, including jackets and gloves, as it can get chilly in the mornings and evenings
Bring sunhats and sunscreen, as the sun can be strong even in winter
Check the road conditions before driving on Ninety Mile Beach, and always follow safety guidelines
Bring a picnic lunch and enjoy eating at one of Northland's many scenic spots
Consider visiting the Hokianga Harbour dunes at low tide, when the beach is at its widest and most accessible
